other_observers: Multiple observers at banding station.; I identified the bird, but it was captured by the banding station personnel.
specific_location: RMBO banding station at Barr Lake SP
description: This bird was captured and banded at the RMBO banding station at Barr Lake SP. When the bird was first brought out of the bag, I immediately grew interested by its size and overall green coloration. This was a decent-sized Empidonax with a medium-long bill, extensive green coloration on the upperparts, yellowish tinge to the underparts, thin white eyering, and distinct pale lores. Meredith McBurney provided the following measurements (all in millimeters). P6 was not emarginated which is a characteristic shown by only Willow, Alder, and Acadian Flycatchers. Wing = 70, tail = 57, wing-tail = 13, bill tip from nares = 8.4, bill width = 6, p10>p5.Behaviors: In the hand at the banding station.Call: None heardPlumage: hatch year
photographer: Photographer: Doug Faulkner, Doug Faulkner, Doug Faulkner, Doug Faulkner, Doug Faulkner
